name: Bard
description: Bards are masters of song, speech, and the magic they contain. You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. The multiverse reverberates with a grand music, and Bards are attuned to it in a way that lets them reshape the world. In the worlds of D&D, words and music are not just vibrations of air, but vocalizations with power all their own. The Bard is a master of the spoken word and instrumental music, channeling power through performance. Bards say that the cosmos is a work of art, for it was spoken into existence. The gods' own words gave it shape, and echoes of these primordial Words of Creation still resound throughout the cosmos. The music of creation still resounds throughout the multiverse, a fundamental magic called the Song of Creation. The cosmos is a work of art, for it was sung and spoken into existence.

weapons: Martial weapons that have the Finesse or Light property
tools: Three Musical Instruments of your choice

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: You can supernaturally inspire others through words, music, or dance. This inspiration is represented by your Bardic Inspiration die, which is a d6. You can use Bardic Inspiration in the following ways: Boost (Reaction): When a creature within 60 feet of you fails a d20 Test, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and roll the Bardic Inspiration die. The creature can add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. Heal (Bonus Action): A creature within 60 feet of you (which can be you) regains a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die plus your Charisma modifier. You can use Bardic Inspiration a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through your bardic arts. You know two cantrips of your choice from the Bard spell list, and you know four level 1 spells of your choice from that list.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description: You've picked up knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Whenever you make an ability check that uses a skill proficiency, you can add half your Proficiency Bonus (rounded down) to the check if the check doesn't already include your Proficiency Bonus. Additionally, whenever you make an ability check that uses Intelligence, and you don't have proficiency in the skill associated with that check, you can add half your Proficiency Bonus (rounded down) to the check.

features.name: Bard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Bard subclass of your choice, which represents the bardic college where you learned your skills.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d8, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: You now regain all expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has Advantage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d10.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You've learned how to cast spells from outside the usual range of Bard spells. Choose two spells from any class's spell list. The spells you choose must be of a level for which you have spell slots. The chosen spells are Bard spells for you but don't count against the number of Bard spells you know.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: Choose two more spells from any class's spell list. Each spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d12.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: Choose two more spells from any class's spell list. Each spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll Initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use of it.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales. Whether singing folk ballads in taverns or elaborate compositions in royal courts, these Bards use their gifts to hold audiences spellbound. When the applause dies down, the audience members might find themselves questioning everything they held to be true, from their faith in the priesthood of the local temple to their loyalty to the king.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

3.name: Cutting Words
3.description: You learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the DM determines whether the attack roll or ability check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage. The creature is immune if it can't hear you or if it's immune to being charmed.

6.name: Additional Magical Secrets
6.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you know.

14.name: Peerless Skill
14.description: When you make an ability check, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration. Roll a Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to your ability check. You can choose to do so after you roll the die for the ability check, but before the DM tells you whether you succeed or fail.

tools: One Musical Instrument of your choice
